arts.ttl: AC Milan vs. Juventus, a big showdown
arts.sum: Juventus have scored just two goals in their last seven Serie A games against AC Milan (from May 2021 onwards); in the same period, among the teams they’ve faced at least three times in the competition, the Rossoneri have conceded fewer goals only against Venezia (zero) – on the other hand, the Bianconeri haven’t scored fewer against any side. Juventus (62) and AC Milan (45) are the two teams with the most sequences of at least 10 passes ending with a shot or a touch in the opponents box in Serie A in 2024/25; the Bianconeri have the highest possession in the current league season (61.6%) while the Rossoneri have won each of their two games that ended with less than 50% possession (against Udinese and Inter).

arts.ttl: Juventus Set Sights on Out-of-Favor Chelsea Midfielder
arts.sum: Serie A giants Juventus have reportedly added Chelsea outcast Cesare Casadei to their list of targets ahead of the winter transfer window. According to Calciomercato, the record-time Italian champions see the 21-year-old as a favorable market opportunity after he tumbled down the pecking order at Stamford Bridge. The post Juventus Set Sights on Out-of-Favor Chelsea Midfielder appeared first on The Cult of Calcio.

arts.ttl: Juventus Eye Zirkzee as Napoli and Atalanta Compete for Raspadori
AC Milan’s Critical Clash Against Juventus: Fonseca’s Last-Minute Lineup Shuffle
Douglas Luiz to Man Utd? Juventus consider SHOCK swap
arts.sum: According to Italian media, Juventus could try to engineer a shock swap with Manchester United that would see Douglas Luiz return to the Premier League while Joshua Zirkzee would head back to Serie A. The Old Lady are interested in Man Utd forward Joshua Zirkzee but know that a January move could prove difficult, so the outlet suggests that they could seek a swap deal with Luiz.

arts.ttl: Juventus eyeing Cesare Casadei as Chelsea midfielder’s future uncertain
arts.sum: Juventus has reignited its interest in Cesare Casadei, the talented 2003-born midfielder currently with Chelsea, after a pause in discussions over recent months. Casadei, who has struggled to secure a place in Enzo Maresca’s plans at Chelsea, could represent a valuable opportunity for the Bianconeri as his playing time diminishes. Calciomercato report how over the past two years, Casadei has been on loan at Reading and Leicester City to gain experience and develop his skills. This summer, he opted to stay at Chelsea, reuniting with his former coach Maresca. However, his season has been disappointing, featuring only four appearances primarily The post Juventus eyeing Cesare Casadei as Chelsea midfielder’s future uncertain appeared first on FootItalia.com.
